what_it_does: Serializes the runtime cognition registries into a JSON-ready /cognition_info payload for a generic frontend live view. It projects file-discovered roles, declared routing rules, edge-kind vocabulary, thought-shape archetypes, activation contexts, rule operations, destination kinds, casts, node-state vocabulary, the per-turn cognition.* event contract, and optional corpus-pillar registries (projections/spaces, lifters, mark types, generation policies, relation types, AI tics, forms).
observations: Defines SCHEMA_VER = 2 and bumps it additively when the serialization shape changes.

Contains COGNITION_EVENT_KINDS, a data declaration of the per-turn events the frontend binds to (cognition.turn.start, cognition.role.fire, cognition.role.ran, cognition.inject, cognition.part, cognition.turn.done).
_serialize_role extracts role identity, spec, facet predicates, op/input_addresses, optional config fields (thinking/tools/knobs/context/model_binding), and declared rules.
_serialize_rule delegates to rule.as_record() when available, otherwise copies a dict-like value.
build_cognition_info is the top-level serializer returning a plain dict with sorted role/rule keys and optional registries defaulting to empty collections.
Fail-loud checks enforce that registry keys match the object's own id for roles, rules, and thought-shape archetypes, and that thought-shape/activation-context values are dicts.
Corpus-pillar registries are passed in and projected via as_records() (and projections.embeddable() for spaces); None means absent/empty.
The module imports nothing from the runtime and relies on duck-typed arguments typed as Any.
standouts: Fail-loud key/id mismatch validation across roles, rules, and thought shapes.
The cognition.* event-kind contract is declared as data inside the serializer, not inferred by the frontend.
Additive SCHEMA_VER with optional corpus-pillar registries so older callers still produce a valid shape.
Heavy use of external design-rule references (06 §F, C9.2, G7) in docstrings.
conventions: Follows the registry-is-truth / one-source principle: UI, runtime, and tools all project from the same registries.
Mirrors contracts/object_info.py as a sibling serializer.
Deterministic output: iterates sorted keys for roles and rules.
Schema-additive evolution: new fields carry defaults; absent optional registries yield empty lists.
Docstrings cite section numbers and rule IDs from design documents.
concerns: Arguments are typed Any, so malformed inputs fail at runtime inside as_records()/embeddable() rather than at the boundary.
No validation that corpus-pillar objects actually expose as_records() or embeddable() before calling them.
Event kind field declarations are string glosses, not a machine-checked schema; drift between emitters and these declarations would not be caught here.
Activation contexts are only checked to be dicts, not validated for required keys.
The module references many external concepts (address system, C6 faculty injections, five destinations) that are not defined in this file.
notes: This file is the backend-to-frontend projection layer for the cognition system: it turns runtime registries into a stable, sorted, validated JSON contract that a generic renderer consumes.
questions: Where are the runtime Role and Rule classes defined, and what exact attributes must they expose?
What code actually emits each cognition.* event and how is that verified against COGNITION_EVENT_KINDS?
What are the exact shapes of THOUGHT_SHAPES and ACTIVATION_CONTEXTS entries beyond being dicts?
How does the frontend use schema_ver to decide which optional registry fields to expect?
What is the address system (ui://cognition/<turn>, run://<turn>/<role>) and where is it implemented?
How are the corpus-pillar registries discovered and passed into build_cognition_info?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file serializes cognition registries for a generic frontend view, enforces one-source identity checks, and declares the event contract, exactly as its docstring claims.
apparent_themes: Spine module that imports nothing above it.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the live cognition view fully data-driven: drop a role file, rule, edge kind, or corpus registry file, restart, and the frontend automatically sees the updated vocabulary without per-change UI code.
novelty: Embedding the per-turn event emit contract inside the serialization module, and extending the same generic-projection pattern to corpus-pillar registries (lenses, lifters, marks, policies, relations, tics, forms) alongside roles and rules.
contribution: It contributes a deterministic, validated, additive serialization of the entire cognition layer (roles, rules, vocabularies, event contract, corpus registries) that a generic frontend can render without per-type code.
summary_for_embedding: company/contracts/cognition_info.py is a deterministic serialization module that builds the /cognition_info payload exposing roles, routing rules, edge kinds, thought shapes, activation contexts, rule operations, destinations, casts, node states, event kinds, and optional corpus-pillar registries for a generic live cognition frontend view.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to be the runtime-to-frontend contract for the cognition layer, mirroring object_info.py so the live view can render all cognition registries generically from one source of truth.
suggested_fields: event_emitter_locations — which source files emit each cognition.* event
registry_source_paths — the file paths or modules that define each passed-in registry
frontend_schema_consumer — the FE component/version that reads schema_ver
rule_cost_deferred_marker — tracking which inject rules still lack a cost field pending C6

symbols.does: Serializes one file-discovered role into a frontend cognition-node record.
symbols.description: Extracts the role's id, spec, facet predicates (can_fire, is_jury, draws, mode_scope), requires, C-build facets (op, input_addresses), optional config fields (thinking, tools, knobs, context, model_binding), and declared rules. It raises ValueError if the registry key disagrees with role.id.

symbols.does: Serializes one declared G3 routing rule into an addressable/renderable record.
symbols.description: Delegates to rule.as_record() when available; otherwise returns a dict built from the input. It raises ValueError if the registry key disagrees with rule.id, mirroring the role validation.

symbols.does: Builds the complete /cognition_info JSON payload from runtime registries.
symbols.description: Collects sorted roles, rules, edge kinds, thought shapes, activation contexts, rule ops, destination kinds, casts, node states, the cognition.* event contract, and optional corpus-pillar registries. It enforces fail-loud identity and shape checks and returns a plain JSON-serializable dict.
